opening_message: I need to move booking BK-1106 to June 27. I would prefer to stay on the same airline if possible, but only if that still makes sense overall. I also do not want to mess up the rest of my day in Atlanta just to satisfy that preference. Please compare the direct-change path against cancel-and-rebook first, and do not cancel or book anything until I approve a specific option. My user ID is user_001.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You have a soft preference to stay on the same airline, but you mainly want the globally best valid move. You expect the agent to compare direct change against cancel-and-rebook first, notice that the cheaper strategy may point to a different airline, and then explain whether your same-airline preference is actually worth the downstream disruption tradeoff. You do not want the agent to treat airline continuity as an instruction to act before comparing, and you still want a final explicit approval gate before any write.
known_info: Current trip: ORD to ATL on 2026-06-23
task_rules: Ask the agent to compare direct change versus cancel-and-rebook before taking action.
If the agent treats your same-airline preference as decisive before comparing the costs, push back and ask for the actual comparison first.

Once the agent identifies cancel-and-rebook as the cheaper path, ask whether that means leaving the same airline and whether the recommendation changes only because of cost or because the same-airline path would also be worse for the rest of your day.
If the agent answers only in airline-loyalty terms, ask for a fuller recap of the actual tradeoff rather than a carrier-only framing.
Do not approve anything until the agent recaps both strategies, explains why the cheaper option requires switching airlines, and asks for a final explicit go-ahead.
Only after that recap, give an explicit final approval to proceed with the cheaper valid option even though it leaves the original airline.
End with [TASK_DONE] once the approved option has been executed and summarized.
For any booking creation or flight change, all booking preferences must end up correct before you accept completion.
Search-stage preferences to verify before approving a booking: airline, time_range, and max_stops.
If the agent has not surfaced enough option detail to verify airline, time_range, and max_stops, ask for that information before approving the booking.
Booking-stage persisted preferences to verify on the final booking are: meal_preference, cabin_class, seat_type, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, and add_insurance.
If the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, answer directly using your profile preferences.

If the agent books with any wrong booking-stage preference, correct exactly one preference at a time in this order: cabin_class, seat_type, meal_preference, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, add_insurance.
Do not try to verify raw flight_id. Budget remains a hard constraint.
Do not end with [TASK_DONE] for a booking creation or flight change until the final booking preferences are correct.

task_summary:
Traveler wants the cheaper valid way to move an existing booking to 2026-06-27 and explicitly asks the agent to compare direct change versus cancel-and-rebook. Correct resolution: cancel and rebook is the cheaper valid path in this scenario because its fee burden is lower than direct change.

Challenge: This tests whether the agent can separate a soft same-airline preference from the globally better strategy, explain the broader trip-disruption tradeoff instead of framing the decision as carrier loyalty alone, and still respect an explicit approval gate before acting.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must compare the direct-change path against the cancel-and-rebook path before executing the user's request.
Agent must recommend and execute cancel and rebook as the cheaper valid option for this booking.
Agent must not frame direct change as the cheaper valid option for this scenario.
Agent must not cancel, create, or update any booking before the user gives an explicit final approval after the comparison recap.
